summary: The maximum concentration of alpha-fetoprotein occurs in amniotic fluid because it is secreted directly by fetal tissues like the yolk sac and liver into this environment. This makes amniotic fluid a primary site for assessing high-level AFP during prenatal testing or diagnostic interventions.
